headline: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
description: TDD (Kent Beck, 2002) is a development practice: write a failing test → write minimum code to pass → refactor. Red-Green-Refactor cycle. TDD ensures testability, drives design toward small, loosely coupled units, and provides a regression safety net. Not about testing — it's about design.

citation.name: Test-Driven Development: By Example (2025 Updated Edition)
citation.author: Beck K.

citation.name: Software Testing in the Age of AI: A 2025 Survey
